## REPORT 6
Title: From the Trading Floor 27-Nov-2025
Created At: 2025-11-28T04:59:30.199Z
Item ID: 69292c32ae4e0b8d02452fb8
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69292c32ae4e0b8d02452fb8.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
November 27, 2025 
• 164k finally breached with the KSE-100 gaining +1.34% DoD  
• 75 shares rose while 21 fell with MEBL PA (+4.42%), LUCK PA (+3.41%) and PPL PA (+3.47%) contributing 
the most to index gains  
• SWCL PA (-0.73%), PIOC PA (-0.52%) and PTC PA (-0.65%) were the biggest index drags  
• Headline inflation for Nov’25 is expected to come in at +6.2% YoY. For 5MFY26, average headline inflation is 
projected to settle around +5.0%, lower than the +7.9% observed during 5MFY25 
• Pakistan and its partners are set to achieve financial close in the next two weeks and secure USD 3.5bn in 
funding for the multibillion-dollar Reko Diq copper and gold mining project 
• The Sindh government has formally approved the strategic partnership submitted by ADM Group China to 
establish more than 600 EV charging stations across the province 
• Heading into the last session of the week the KSE-100 is currently +2.02% WoW and 164k will now need to 
build as support to set up moves back to the Oct highs 
KSE-100 Daily Chart

•

•

drags

the upside draw

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
September 29, 2025 
• Solid start to the week with the KSE-100 gaining +0.98% DoD with intraday high coming in @ 163.9k 
• 60 shares rose while 40 fell with FFC PA (+3.1%), PSO PA (+6.16%) and HBL PA (+2.51%) 
contributing the most to index gains  
• ENGROH PA (-0.66%), LUCK PA (-0.73%) and DGKC PA (-2.52%) were the biggest index drags 
• GAL PA (+0.04%) announced FY25 EPS: PKR 71.85, +11x YoY and DPS: PKR 10 which was above 
expectations  
• AIRLINK PA (+10.0%) announced FY25 EPS: 12.01, +3% YoY and DPS: 7.0 also beating 
expectations 
• Weekly draw remains @ 165k with support rising to 162k

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
August 29, 2025 
• 
Friday recovery to keep 147.5k in tact heading into the weekend to close -0.6% WoW 
• 
61 shares rose while 38 with HBL PA (+3.12%), DGKC PA (+10.0%) and LUCK PA (+2.19%) 
contributing the most to index gains  
• 
ENGROH PA (-0.95%), MEBL PA (-1.03%) and INDU PA (-4.84%) were the biggest index drags  
•

• 
INDU PA announced FY25 EPS: PKR 292.74, +53% YoY and DPS: 176 
• 
SBP has announced NBP PA, UBL PA, and HBL PA as domestic systemically important banks for 
2025 
• 
Recovery from an area of interest keeps the view in tact with 151.2k the draw for the coming week 
with support @ 147.5k

## REPORT 10
Title: From the Trading Floor 31-Jul-2025
Created At: 2025-07-31T11:28:08.463Z
Item ID: 688b5348aa3685d1183f16f0
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
July 31, 2025 
• 
Supply above 140k continues to cap gains for the KSE-100 
• 
60 shares rose while 39 fell with SYS PA (+7.5%), OGDC PA (+3.66%), POL PA (+5.02%) contributed the 
most to index gains  
• 
FFC PA (-1.41%), BAFL PA (-2.1%) and EFERT PA (-0.73%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
Oil names were all well bid and saw the most value traded following comments from President Trump that 
his administration has struck a deal with Pakistan in which Washington will work with Islamabad in 
developing the South Asian nation’s oil reserves. There was no update on tariffs which the market continues 
to eagerly wait for  
• 
BAFL PA (-2.33%) announced 1HCY25 EPS: 9.55, -31% YoY and DPS 5.0  
• 
HBL PA (-0.58%) announced 1HCY25 EPS: 23.44, +16% YoY and DPS 9.0 
• 
The strong gap at the start of the session sees near term support rise to 138.6k-139.4k and heading into the 
last session of the week the KSE-100 is currently +0.13% WoW with the 140.5k upside draw still open

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
June 27, 2025 
• 
Very strong week for Pakistan with the KSE-100 +3.6% WoW to close above 124k 
• 
On Friday the KSE-100 gained +1.91% with FFC PA (+2.23%), LUCK PA (+4.28%) and 
MEBL PA (+2.98%) contributing the most to index gains  
• 
PSEL PA (-8.3%), NATF PA (-1.76%), ISL PA (-0.8%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
PM Sharif and US Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke and agreed to continue working 
closely to strengthen bilateral relations, particularly through enhanced trade 
• 
GoP has received commercial loans of USD 3.1bn and multilateral loans of more than USD 
500mn, which will be reflected in total reserves announcement later today 
• 
POL PA (+6.06%) and MOL find deposits from Makori Deep-03 in KP which will produce 
22mcf/d gas and 2,112 barrels/day oil 
• 
For the coming week support is anticipated around 122k and 127k will be the upside draw

• 
Gains to close out the week (+0.5% WoW) but, still below the 120k

coming budget

including Cherat Cement Co. and Bestway Group 
•

## REPORT 17
Title: From the Trading Floor - 31-Dec-24
Created At: 2024-12-31T11:52:09.143Z
Item ID: 6773dae9ecfd7de5dde48013
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
December 31, 2024 
• 
Year end selling kept the KSE-100 below the recent highs  
• 
49 shares rose while 50 fell with PPL PA (+2.3%), UBL PA (+1.03%) and HBL PA (+1.7%) contributed the most 
to index gains  
• 
FFC PA (-1.51%), MCB PA (-2.1%) and BAFL PA (-2.1%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
According to media reports, the government has approved the sale of a 15% stake in the Reko Diq Project to 
Saudi Arabia for USD 540mn (~PKR 150bn). Saudi Arabia is expected to make the payment in two phases: USD 
330mn in the Phase-I and the remaining USD 210mn in the Phase-II. Federal Government’s stake is held through 
OGDC, PPL, and GHPL, with each holding an 8.33% stake. In the event of the sale of stake, OGDC and PPL are 
expected to book gain of investment of PKR 2.56/share and PKR 4.12/share, respectively. Additionally, the cash 
flow impact is projected at PKR 11.63/share for OGDC and PKR 18.39/share for PPL. 
• 
The KSE-100 closed out the year with a gain of +88% (USD) to rank #2 globally  
• 
Heading into the new year the KSE-100 averages +4% in January over the last 10-yrs  
•

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
October 31, 2024 
• 
Nice push lower from resistance with the KSE-100 giving up -1.23% DoD to close the month below 90k 
• 
MTD the KSE-100 gained +9.93% in October, 2nd globally behind Kenya 
• 
27 shares rose while 72 fell with banks having the largest drag on the index  
• 
Pakistan achieved a budget surplus for the first time since 2QFY04. A remarkable profit of PKR 2.5trn from the 
SBP was the primary driver of this surplus. The primary surplus hit a historic high of PKR 3 trn, the largest ever 
recorded. 
• 
While corrections can be scary and many consistently call a top, Pakistan remains in a bull market, and in the 
strongest seasonal period of the year 
• 
Corrections remain buying opportunities as the market approaches 88k

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
September 27, 2024 
• 
Intra week of 82,995 before closing down -0.9% WoW 
• 
On Friday 29 shares rose while 69 fell with the largest downside contributions coming from MARI PA (-2.55%), 
UBL PA (-1.72%) and ENGRO PA (-1.66%)  
• 
Pakistan first IMF review meeting is expected in March/April  
• 
According to the MoF Exports in Sept. are likely in range of USD 2.5-USD 3bn, imports USD 4.5-USD 5bn and 
workers’ remittances USD 2.7-USD3.2bn 
• 
Headline inflation for Sep '24 projected at +7.3% YoY—the lowest since Jan '21 
• 
For the coming week the market is expected to find support in the 80-81k zone and resume upside moves

## REPORT 21
Title: From Trading Floor 29-Aug-2024
Created At: 2024-08-29T14:25:13.757Z
Item ID: 66d084c9311a3f7dcc92b252
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66d084c9311a3f7dcc92b252.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
August 29, 2024 
• 
The KSE-100 continues to carve out a bottom around the 78k level. 
• 
44 stocks rose while 55 fell with MTL PA (+9.62%), ENGRO PA (+4.05%) and NBP PA (+8.14%) the biggest 
contributors to index gains.  
• 
MEBL PA (-1.02%), SYS PA (-1.47%) and EFERT PA (-0.9%) had the largest index drag. 
• 
In a ground breaking development, NBP PA has reported an extraordinary item of PKR 49bn in 2QCY24 related to the 
settlement of a pension case. This amounts to a per-share impact of PKR 12.0 (after tax). Since 2016, the bank had 
suspended dividend payouts while awaiting the outcome of the pension case. This suspension led to a substantial 
increase in Total CAR and Tier-1 CAR from 16.5% and 12.3% in CY16 (the last time dividends were distributed) to 
24.85% and 18.7% in 1QCY24, both comfortably exceeding the minimum requirements of 14% and 10.5%. 
• 
Looking forward to CY25, we project improved earnings of PKR 18.5/share for NBP, unburdened by one-time events. 
This is likely to result in a higher payout, with our base case assuming a 50% payout, equating to PKR 9/share. This 
positive outlook further reinforces NBP as an attractive investment, supported by its solid fundamental strengths. 
• 
While the market is rising from support the rally is lethargic rather than dynamic, participation of cement stocks here is 
needed to give the move legs.

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
July 31, 2024 
 
• 
Price followed through on Tuesday’s declines with additional downside to close below 78k 
• 
31 shares rose and 68 fell, FFC PA (+0.96%), FATIMA PA (+3.79%) and ENGRO PA (+0.58%) provided the 
biggest upside contributions while UBL PA (-2.13%), SYS PA (-2.96%) and LUCK PA (-2.72%) were the largest 
downside contributors  
• 
The KSE-100 is on in the 77.5-78K weekly support zone, if the market is bullish this is where buyers should 
emerge 
• 
80k needs to be regained to signal that a low is place and open up upside potential  
• 
Several key stocks are at important levels and the remainder of week should dictate if those levels hold and the 
market rallies or if they give way to indicate 82k was an interim top

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
June 25, 2024 
 
• 
More declines as the KSE-100 moves down to test support  
• 
30 shares rose while 65 fell with the largest positive contributions coming from ENGRO PA (+2.1%), UNITY PA 
(8.05%) and FATIMA PA (+5.0%)  
• 
MCB PA (-2.28%), OGDC PA (-1.97%) and HUBC PA (-0.73%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
Bidding for potential PIA bidders will be held in the first week of August  
• 
Pakistan is making positive progress with the IMF according to FinMin 
• 
A new a security operation to contain a surge in terrorist attacks that have targeted China’s infrastructure projects 
and its citizens as Islamabad seeks to bolster economic ties with Beijing. 
• 
KSE-100 and KSE-30 are now approaching near term support levels from where upside moves can be 
anticipated

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
February 26, 2024 
 
▪ 
Price drew into the “General Election Gap” on the first trading day of the week  
▪ 
71 shares rose while 25 fell with POL PA (+2.53%), MEBL PA (+1.36%), and SYS PA (+1.36%) being the biggest 
contributors to index gains  
▪ 
Tech names remained well bid with NETSOL PA (+7.5%), AIRLINK PA (+5.6%), and TRG PA (+2.45%) 
▪ 
ENGRO PA (+0.62%) announced CY23 EPS: PKR 39.17, -14% YoY, and DPS: 48. For the quarter, the ENGRO PA 
booked LPS: PKR 3.22 due to a loss on Thermal Assets re-measurement. It is pertinent to note that this loss on re-
measurement will not result in cash outflow and when the transaction of sale of Thermal assets concludes, the difference 
between the agreed sale price and the cost of investment (gain on divestment) will be reflected in standalone accounts 
of ENGRO PA  
▪ 
PPL PA (-0.79%) 1HFY24 EPS: PKR 17.98, +41% YoY. For 2QFY24, PPL PA posted its highest ever EPS: PKR 14.92, 
+75% YoY. DPS PKR 2.50/share in 2QFY24. 
▪ 
The 63-64k zone is a congestion area for the KSE-100 and focus should remain on tech names this week

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
June 27, 2023 
 
• The short week concluded today with a range bound session at the PSX. The benchmark KSE-100 index opened negative after the SBP raised interest rates by 100bps in a 
last-ditch MPC meeting yesterday in an attempt to resume the stalled IMF program. The market traded in the red for the majority of the day, but it ended in the green as 
investors chose to add value to their portfolios. Across the board, volumes remained healthy. 
• The index closed at 41,452.68, up by 15.58pts (+0.04% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Commercial Banks (+120.1pts), Power Generation & Distribution 
(+9.7pts), Tobacco (+9.7pts), Engineering (+9.4pts) and Inv. Banks / Inv. Cos. / Securities Cos. (+5.9pts).  
• Volumes increased from 226.85mn shares to 234.7mn shares (+3.5% DoD). The average traded value also increased by 7.0% to USD 26.2mn as against USD 24.6mn. 
• Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes were BIPL, WTL, OGDC, PIBTL and KEL.

Pakistan

May 31, 2023 
 
• A negative session was observed at the PSX today. The benchmark KSE-100 index began the day in green, but bulls lost control as the bears triumphed at the end of the day. 
The KSE-100 hit an intraday low of 41,265 (-406.62 points)  following some market chatter with regard to new tax measures in the upcoming budget as investors decided to 
book profits. Investor participation was marginally lower while the cement sector remained in the limelight. 
• The index closed at 41,330.56 down by 341.09pts (-0.82% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Technology & Communication (-86.6pts), Cement (-67.9pts), 
E&P’s (-51.5pts), Chemical (-27.0pts), and Fertilizer (-18.9pts).  
• Volumes decreased from 197.7mn shares to 158.1mn shares (-20.0% DoD). The average traded value also decreased by 4.6% to USD 21.4mn as against USD 22.5mn. 
• Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes were WTL, MLCF, FCCL, TRG, and CNERGY.

• The PSX closed the business week with a range-bound trading session today. The market started in the green and went on to trade both ways as bulls and bears battled for 
control of the bourse throughout the day. The continuing political turmoil and the delay in signing the SLA for the IMF's Extended Fund Facility kept investors at bay. However, 
fresh buying was seen across the board during the last trading hour, as the index ended in the green. Main board volumes remained dull while 3rd tier stocks led the volume 
board. 
• The index closed at 40,000.83 pts, up by 152.5pts (+0.38% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Commercial Banks (+39.4pts), Cement (+28.1pts), Fertilizer

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
October 31, 2022 
 
 
Despite the lack of investors’ participations a positive session was witnessed at the PSX today. Prevailing political clatter kept the volumes dry in the main board as 
3rd tier stocks continued to be the volumes leaders. 
 
The Index closed at 41,264.66pts, up by 124.33pts (0.30% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Technology & Communication (46.7pts), Power 
Generation & Distribution (32.5pts), Cement (21.5pts), E&P’s (16.2pts) and Automobile Assembler (12.1pts).  
 
Volumes decreased from 177.7mn shares to 97.5mn shares (-45.2% DoD). The average traded value also decreased by -45.9% to USD 11.3mn as against USD 
20.9mn. 
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are WTL, GTECH, SILK, KEL and CNERGY.

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
September 30, 2022 
 
 
The market remained under pressure all day long as investors participated left and right. The PSX remained sluggish during the opening half but got active in the second trading 
session. Investors maintained a strategic distance due to political and economic instability, however due to rollover week, hefty volumes were witnessed in the future contracts that led 
the index close in the green.  
 
The Index closed at 41,128.67pts, up by 114.81pts (+0.28% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Technology (+80.4pts), E&P’s (+35.6pts), Fertilizer (+24.4pts), 
Investment Banks (+20.9pts) and Tobacco (+13.0pts).  
 
Volumes increased from 127.4mn shares to 205.7mn shares (61.4% DoD). The average traded value also increased by 72.1% to US$ 41.6mn as against US$ 24.2mn. 
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are TRG, TPLP, KEL, PIBTL and CNERGY.

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
August 31, 2022 
 
 
The market opened in the negative zone today due to prevailing political noise. The benchmark KSE-100 index witnessed a range bound session today as investor remained on the 
side line awaiting for clarity on the political front. However, during the last trading hour value buying was observed in the cement sector which helped the index to close in the green 
zone. Volumes remained dry in the main board although hefty volumes were witnessed in the 3rd tier stocks 
 
The Index closed at 42,351.15pts, up by 155.89pts (+0.37% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Cements (+75.3pts), Chemicals (+32.3pts), Fertilizers (+23.1pts), 
Power (+22.9pts) and E&P’s (+18.7pts).  
 
Volumes decreased from 237.6mn shares to 181.3mn shares (-23.7% DoD). Average traded value also decreased by 1.5% to reach US$ 33.0mn as against US$ 33.4mn. 
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are KEL, UNITY, EPCL, FCCL and WTL.

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
July 28, 2022 
 
 
PSX witnessed a positive session today. The KSE-100 index traded in the green zone throughout the day as investors opted for value hunting across the board. Rally was observed 
in the IT sector as IT exports have surged to $ 2.616 bn (FY 2021-22). Investors participation remained healthy as hefty volumes were witnessed in the main board and 3rd tier stocks. 
 
The Index closed at 40,276.64pts, up by 304.00pts (+0.76% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (+154.1pts), Technology (+93.3pts), E&P’s (+64.9pts), Power 
(+24.7pts) and OMC’s (+18.5pts).  
 
Volumes increased from 121.6mn shares to 251.3mn shares (+106.7% DoD). Average traded value also increased by 125.3% to reach US$ 39.0mn as against US$ 17.3mn.  


From Trading Floor

Stock Market                                                    
May 31, 2022 
 
 
The market remained range bound throughout the day, due to concerns over rising inflation number and tightening of money supply. The benchmark KSE-100 index opened in the 
positive zone but lackluster activity was witnessed. Volumes remained dry in the main board although hefty volumes were witnessed in the 3rd tier stocks. 
 
The Index closed at 43,078.14pts, up by 38.00pts (+0.09% DOD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Power (+64.2pts), E&P’s (+56.8pts), Technology (+21.7pts), Autos 
(+20.1pts) and Fertilizer (+16.4pts).  


Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
April 27, 2022 
 
PSX observed a range bound secession today, KSE-100 index remained volatile throughout the day due to expectation of rate hike in upcoming policy as KIBOR reached 
13years high. On the contrary, rally in the refinery sector was witnessed due to rumors regarding the approval of refinery policy. The last trading hour witnessed profit selling 
across the board which resulted in the secession ending in the red zone. Main board activity remained dull although hefty volumes were witnessed in the 3rd tier stocks. 
 
The Index closed at 45,533.30pts, down by 255.58pts (-0.62% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Fertilizer (-106.8pts), Technology (-61.7pts), Cement (-
39.5pts), and Chemicals (-34.8pts).  


•

After the initial uptick of 67pts carrying positive sentiment from yesterday, market took a nose dive due to concerns on foreign policy front where US announced sanctions on 
Taliban and its supporters that had PSX investors worry on the fate of relations with US. Resultantly, the index tumbled 1300pts during the session and closed the session -
1119pts (unadjusted). Selling was observed across the board with major decline in Technology, Banks, E&P, O&GMCs and Fertilizer. Among scrips, TELE topped the 
volumes with 52.4M shares, followed by WTL (50.8M) and DCR (36M).

80.1mn.

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
April 28, 2026 
• Spill over impact of a larger than expected hike in rates by the SBP kept the market under pressure 
throughout the day  
• 29 shares rose while 70 fell with ENGROH PA (+0.97%), PAKT PA (+8.52%) and POL PA (+1.39%) 
contributing the most to index gains  
• UBL PA (-2.25%), FFC PA (-1.5%) and PPL PA (-1.36%) were the biggest index drags  
• US President Donald Trump convened his national security team to discuss Iran’s proposal to end a war now 
in its third month. Iran has signalled it may be willing to accept an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz 
in exchange for Washington ending its blockade of Iranian ports.

and DPS: 1.5. Both earnings were inline with expectations however some market participants had been 
anticipating blow out earnings, hence the selling pressure post announcement

March 30, 2026 
• Back to seeing a large drawdown on Monday with the KSE-100 losing another -3.21% to close below the 
150k level  
• 2 shares rose while 97 fell with FFC PA (-2.95%), ENGROH PA (-3.68%) and MEBL PA (-3.84%) were the

cashflow and provide an exit opportunity to those who wish to liquidate their investments 
• Middle East news flow continues to supress risk appetite with investors still hesitant to allocate despite some 
markets trading higher today    
• The KSE-100 is now approaching the low from earlier in the month and without some positive news flow is in 
danger of being taken out 
• As the benchmark indices approach these lows long term investors should continue to accumulate at 
depressed levels

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
January 28, 2026 
• Another session of range bound behaviour around the key 188k level  
• 42 shares rose while 56 fell with UBL PA (+1.84%), PPL PA (+2.91%) and PIOC PA (+10.0%) contributing 
the most to index gains  
• FFC PA (-1.52%), MARI PA (-1.15%) and HUBC PA (-0.85%) were the biggest index drags  
• LUCK PA (+0.83%) reported 1HFY26 EPS of PKR 30.45/share, +13% YoY, driven primarily by the strong 
performance of local cement operations which are increased by +8% YoY. In addition, foreign cement 
operations continued to support profitability, while the automobile and mobile phone segments recorded 
improvements in volumes 
• In 2025, Pakistan's mineral exports to China recorded strong and diversified growth with Copper exports 
crossing USD 1.14bn 
• Pakistan’s exports to the USA remained largely flat in December of the current fiscal year compared with the 
same month last year, despite benefiting from lower tariff rates 
• SBP purchased USD 1.03bn from the interbank market in October, similar to the previous month, to build its 
foreign exchange reserves and make external debt repayments 
• Support remains @ 185k with 191k still in sight for this week

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
November 24, 2025 
• Flat session to start the new trading week with the KSE-100 continuing to trade either side of 162k 
• 38 shares rose while 59 fell with FFC PA (+1.47%), ENGROH PA (+1.62%) and PIOC PA (+5.45%) 
contributing the most to index gains  
• MARI PA (-1.5%), OGDC PA (-1.28%) and MEBL PA (-1.06%) were the biggest index drags  
• Pakistan’s food import bill soared to USD 3.075bn, +32% YoY. The surge highlights growing reliance on imp-
orted food commodities amid domestic production and supply challenges. The increase was primarily driven by 
higher imports of sugar, edible oil, and tea 
• PPL PA (-1.16%) will drill its first offshore oil well in March 2026 along Sindh’s southern shoreline, in the Sirani 
Block near Sujawal about 30kms from the mainland 
• Import of semi and completely knocked down kits by the local assemblers jumped by +123% YoY in 4MFY26. 
Similarly, the import of CBU new and used cars also rose by +31% to USD 113mn in 4MFY26  
• The market could be shaping up to make another run @ 164k this week which has proven to be stubborn 
resistance over the last few weeks

Title: From the Trading Floor - 30-Dec-24
Created At: 2024-12-30T11:46:50.061Z
Item ID: 6772882aecfd7de5dd8a9d26

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
December 30, 2024 
• 
Very strong start to the week with the KSE-100 gaining +3.51% to close back towards the upper end of the 
current range  
• 
87 shares rose while 12 fell with HBL PA (+9.81%), DAWH PA (+10.0%) and MCB PA (+6.83%) contributing the 
most to index gains  
• 
FFC PA (-0.62%), TRG PA (-4.21%) and COLG PA (-1.13%) were the largest index drags  
• 
Pakistan's federal cabinet has approved an ordinance that revises the tax structure for the country's banking 
sector. According to media sources, the new policy eliminates the ADR-related tax on income from government 
securities (10-16%), which had previously been imposed on banks. At the same time, the ordinance raises the 
corporate tax rate for banks. 
• 
The National Accounts Committee (NAC) has released GDP growth rates for 1QFY25 and revised rate for FY24. 
1QFY25 achieved a growth rate of +0.92% YoY. The growth rate for FY24 has been revised slightly downward to 
+2.50%, from +2.52% 
•

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
November 20, 2024 
• 
Supply above 96k saw the KSE-100 retreat -0.3% DoD 
• 
28 shares rose while 70 fell with the biggest downside contributions coming from SYS PA (-4%), HBL PA (-
3.02%) and LUCK PA (-1.7%) 
• 
A Pakistani court granted bail to former PM Imran Khan in a case where he is accused of hiding his income. The 
Islamabad High Court ordered to free Khan from jail, however it is not clear whether the former PM will be 
released from jail, where he has been for more than a year facing charges in more than a hundred cases  
• 
Pakistan’s banks are adding new fees on large accounts to discourage inflows of deposits as they seek to boost 
their ADR to avoid a punitive tax from the government. 
• 
Support remains @ 94k for the KSE-100

Item ID: 672375958f84bab98c2b92f7
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/672375958f84bab98c2b92f7.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
October 31, 2024 
• 
Nice push lower from resistance with the KSE-100 giving up -1.23% DoD to close the month below 90k 
• 
MTD the KSE-100 gained +9.93% in October, 2nd globally behind Kenya 
• 
27 shares rose while 72 fell with banks having the largest drag on the index  
• 
Pakistan achieved a budget surplus for the first time since 2QFY04. A remarkable profit of PKR 2.5trn from the 
SBP was the primary driver of this surplus. The primary surplus hit a historic high of PKR 3 trn, the largest ever 
recorded. 
• 
While corrections can be scary and many consistently call a top, Pakistan remains in a bull market, and in the 
strongest seasonal period of the year 
• 
Corrections remain buying opportunities as the market approaches 88k

## REPORT 80
Title: From The Trading Floor 25-Sep-24
Created At: 2024-09-25T11:46:51.789Z
Item ID: 66f3f82b8f84bab98cb2d5bd
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
September 25, 2024 
• 
Pakistan is poised for upside acceleration with the KSE-100 targeting 86k 
• 
72 shares rose while 25 fell with MCB PA (+4.8%), OGDC PA (+3.22%) and NBP PA (+5.35%) the biggest 
contributors to index gains  
• 
FFC PA (-1.25%), HUBC PA (-0.68%) and FFBL PA (-1.11%) were the largest index drags  
• 
Cements sharply higher with FCCL PA (+3.95%), MLCF PA (+3.05%) and DGKC PA (+1.87%)  
• 
Later today the IMF Executive Board will consider Pakistan’s 37-month EFF of about USD 7bn following the SLA in 
July. FinMin stated “We are confident in our ongoing reform agenda, and economic stability is returning to 
Pakistan, which will lead to the Fund’s approval of the loan program.” 
• 
Approval is likely to spur bullish sentiment and push the KSE-100 towards its 86k target  
• 
Continue to look higher while support levels remain in tact

## REPORT 81
Title: From Trading Floor 28-Aug-2024
Created At: 2024-08-28T13:32:45.263Z
Item ID: 66cf26fd311a3f7dcc49482b
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66cf26fd311a3f7dcc49482b.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
August 28, 2024 
• 
Flat session with the KSE-100 hugging 78k. 
• 
29 shares rose while 65 fell with MARI PA (+2.67%), ENGRO PA (+1.73%), and FFC PA (+1.03%) were the biggest 
contributors to index gains.  
• 
BAHL PA (-0.98%), HINOON PA (-3.56%) and PKGS PA (-4.79%) had the largest index drag. 
• 
Moody’s has upgraded Pakistan’s long-term foreign debt rating to Caa2 from Caa3. This upgrade reflects Pakistan's 
improving macroeconomic conditions and moderately better government liquidity and external positions, from very 
weak levels. 
• 
FABL PA (+1.74%), 2QCY24 EPS: PKR 4.6, +60% YoY | +5% QoQ. For 1HCY24, total earnings rose +77% YoY. 
The rise in earnings is fueled by a jump in total income. Alongside the results the bank also announced a cash 
dividend of PKR 2/share in 2QCY24 taking total payout for 1HCY24 to PKR 3/share. 
• 
Continuing to look for upside moves from the 78k level.

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
July 31, 2024 
 
• 
Price followed through on Tuesday’s declines with additional downside to close below 78k 
• 
31 shares rose and 68 fell, FFC PA (+0.96%), FATIMA PA (+3.79%) and ENGRO PA (+0.58%) provided the 
biggest upside contributions while UBL PA (-2.13%), SYS PA (-2.96%) and LUCK PA (-2.72%) were the largest 
downside contributors  
• 
The KSE-100 is on in the 77.5-78K weekly support zone, if the market is bullish this is where buyers should 
emerge 
• 
80k needs to be regained to signal that a low is place and open up upside potential  
• 
Several key stocks are at important levels and the remainder of week should dictate if those levels hold and the 
market rallies or if they give way to indicate 82k was an interim top

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
June 24, 2024 
 
• 
Monday saw declines following last week’s test of 80k. 
• 
21 shares rose while 75 fell with HUBC PA (+4.33%) providing the largest upside contribution while UBL PA (-
2.57%), MEBL PA (-2.94%) and BAHL PA (-3.29%) were the biggest index drags.  
• 
First support for the KSE-100 comes in around 76-77k and for the KSE-30 @ 25k above which immediate upside 
remains on the table.  
• 
The key levels for the week remain 75k and 24k for the KSE-100 and KSE-30 respectively.

## REPORT 89
Title: From Trading Floor 26-Dec-2023
Created At: 2023-12-26T12:35:36.417Z
Item ID: 658ac898844813743d6ceaa8
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=400&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/658ac898844813743d6ceaa8.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
December 26, 2023 
 
• 
Additional downside to start the week now brings Pakistan into some longer term levels.  
• 
60k KSE-100 and 20K KSE-30 are significant levels and we are now on alert to watch for signs of bottoming and the 
resumption of upside. 
• 
For the remainder of the week we will be watching for the main indices KSE100, KSE30 and KSEALL to show signs of 
divergence to signal a tradable low is in place. 
• 
The drawdown for the KSE-100 has now extended beyond -10% which is historically the turning point in bull markets. 
• 
One point of concern is that Pakistan is currently on the watch list from FTSE for potential demotion from Secondary Emerging 
to Frontier market status. Over the last few years, Pakistan has experienced a steady decrease in its index weight within 
FTSE Russell global benchmarks. This has resulted in the market failing to meet the minimum investable market capitalization 
exit level threshold required to retain a Secondary Emerging market status.  
• 
Based on data as of the close on the 30 June 2023, the total investable market capitalization of Pakistan in the FTSE Emerging 
All Cap index was USD 3.01bn, while the minimum investable market capitalization exit level threshold, required to retain 
Secondary Emerging market status, is USD 3.49bn, as per the Nov FMR Pakistan’s investable market cap stood at USD 
3.788bn. We are currently a touch below the Nov 30 closing. 
• 
To mitigate the FTSE downgrade risk Pakistan needs to rally into year end. 
KSE-100 and KSE-30 Daily

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
June 26, 2023 
 
 Today, the Bulls triumphed at the PSX. The week began with great hopes at the PSX for the Resumption of the IMF's 9th review of the Extended Fund Facility after the Prime 
Minister met with MD IMF over the weekend in an attempt to revive the halted program. Following this development, the SBP announced that import restrictions would be lifted 
to help the industrial sector. Investors were active throughout the day, with substantial purchasing seen across the board. Overall, healthy volumes were recorded. 
 The index closed at 41,437.10, up by 1371.78pts (+3.42% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Commercial Banks (+332.6pts), Fertilizer (+193.3pts), Power 
Generation & Distribution (+145.4pts), Cement (+138.6pts) and Technology & Communication (+117.1pts).  
 Volumes increased from 136.95mn shares to 226.85mn shares (+65.7% DoD). The average traded value also increased by 108.8% to USD 24.6mn as against USD 11.8mn. 
 Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes were WTL, FFBL, SNGP, OGDC and TPLP.

Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
May 30, 2023 
 
• Another productive session was recorded at the PSX today. The market began with sustained bullish sentiments from the previous close as the bulls stayed active throughout 
the day. The PSX soared when the Finance Minister indicated that the government was on track to resume negotiations with the IMF for the long-overdue 9th review of the 
Extended Fund Facility. Investor activity was reasonable, with moderate volumes across the board, with third-tier companies continuing to dominate the volume board. 
• The index closed at 41,671.65 up by 331.59pts (+0.80% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Cement (+95.5pts), E&P’s (+69.5pts), Technology &

• The PSX closed the business week with a range-bound trading session today. The market started in the green and went on to trade both ways as bulls and bears battled for 
control of the bourse throughout the day. The continuing political turmoil and the delay in signing the SLA for the IMF's Extended Fund Facility kept investors at bay. However,

board.



Item ID: 649135897cf4c3f30fbb30f9
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=600&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/649135897cf4c3f30fbb30f9.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
November 29, 2022 
 
• 
A positive session was witnessed at the PSX today. The Index was driven by the bulls throughout the day which shows signs of recovery after yesterday’s sharp decline. 
Investors’ sentiments improved as Govt of Pakistan received US$ 500 million from the Asian infrastructure investment Bank.  Investor participation remained active throughout 
the day, with 3rd tier stocks witnessing the most activity today as well. 
• 
The Index closed at 42,373.59 pts, up by 302.25pts (+0.72% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Commercial Banks (+67.7pts), Technology & 
Communication (+60.7pts), Power Generation & Distribution (+57.5pts), E&P’s(+34.2pts), Cement (+28.5pts).  
• 
Volumes decreased from 244.4mn shares to 139.2mn shares (-43.0% DoD). The average traded value also decreased by 27.5% to USD 22.6mn as against USD 31.1mn. 
• 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are WTL, UNITY,  KEL, BAFL, DFML, GCIL, & HUBC

## REPORT 103
Title: From Trading Floor 27-Oct-2022
Created At: 2022-10-28T13:53:15Z
Item ID: 64914c427cf4c3f30fbefff3
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=600&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/64914c427cf4c3f30fbefff3.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
October 27, 2022 
 
• 
A range bound session was witnessed at the PSX today. The benchmark KSE-100 index started off in the green, but the continuous political unrest kept investors 
away, causing the index to hit an intraday low of 221.37 points. On the other hand, value buying was seen in the final hour of trading, which led the index to close in 
the green. Volumes remained respectable due to rollover week, with 3rd tier stocks continuing to lead in terms of volume. 
• 
The Index closed at 41,602.85pts, up by 62.82pts (0.15% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Technology & Communication (65.5pts), Fertilizer 
(25.9pts), Cement (19.4pts), Commercial Banks (18.5pts) and Chemical (12.4pts).  
• 
Volumes deccreased from 265.4mn shares to 203.5mn shares (-23.3% DoD). The average traded value  also decreased by -17.8% to USD 28.7mn as against USD 
34.9mn. 
• 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are WTL, DFML, TRG, KEL and HUMNL..

## REPORT 104
Title: From Trading Floor 29-Sep-2022
Created At: 2022-09-29T12:37:20Z
Item ID: 64915ccd7cf4c3f30fc120b2
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=600&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/64915ccd7cf4c3f30fc120b2.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
September 29, 2022 
 
 
The benchmark KSE-100 index witnessed a bloodbath session today due to current political clamour and the negative news pertaining to HBL. As investors opted to remain on side 
line from the market because of hazy economic forecast, the market opened in the red and remained under pressure for the rest of the day making an intra-day low of -524.31 points. 
The volumes declined significantly across the main board on the contrary decent volumes were observed in the 3rd tier stocks.  
 
The Index closed at 41,013.86pts, down by 421.27pts (-1.02% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (-246.8pts), Technology (-53.0pts), E&Ps (-26.8pts), 
Chemicals (-21.7pts) and Fertilizers (-17.8pts).  
 
Volumes decreased from 220.6mn shares to 127.4mn shares (-42.2% DoD). The average traded value also decreased by 31.3% to US$ 24.0mn as against US$ 35.0mn. 
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are KEL, TPLP, TRG, NCL and FCCL.

## REPORT 105
Title: From Trading Floor 30-Aug-2022
Created At: 2022-08-30T13:09:06Z
Item ID: 64916e3a7cf4c3f30fc2ea83
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=700&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
August 30, 2022 
 
 
The market opened in the positive zone as the International Monetary Fund’s executive board approved the extended loan facility for Pakistan. However, later in the day investors 
opted for profit taking across the board which made the index to close in the red. Main board volumes remained decent although hefty volumes were witnessed in the 3rd tier stocks. 
 
The Index closed at 42,195.25pts, down by 309.09pts (-0.73% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (-96.0pts), Fertilizer (-48.9pts), Cements (-34.0pts), E&P’s 
(-31.85pts) and Chemicals (-30.5pts).  
 
Volumes increased from 229.2mn shares to 237.6mn shares (+3.7% DoD). Average traded value also increased by 0.4% to reach US$ 33.2mn as against US$ 33.1mn. 
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are KEL, WTL, UNITY, FCCL and PRL.

## REPORT 106
Title: From Trading Floor 27-Jul-2022
Created At: 2022-07-27T12:06:13Z
Item ID: 6491eb0b54aca318bf8b39eb
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=700&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
July 27, 2022 
 
 
PSX continued to remain under pressure throughout the day due to concerns regarding uncertain economic situation. The benchmark KSE-100 index witnessed a volatile session as 
investors opted to remain sideways as Pak Rupee continued its freefall against US Dollar. Main board volumes remained dry whereas decent volumes were observed in the 3rd-tier 
stocks. 
 
The Index closed at 39,972.64pts, up by 78.59pts (+0.20% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (+76.9pts), Chemicals (+54.2pts), Technology (+27.5pts), 
Engineering (+14.4pts) and Cement (+10.1pts).  
 
Volumes decreased from 131.8mn shares to 121.6mn shares (-7.6% DoD). Average traded value also decreased by 1.3% to reach US$ 17.6mn as against US$ 17.8mn.  
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are LOTCHEM, WTL, TPLP, CNERGY and UNITY.

• 
Another sluggish day for the Pakistan Stock Exchange, market opened positive but could not sustain the green zone and the KSE 100 index closed negative 313 points 
during the trading day. Main reasons behind the dull market are political noise, start of future roll over and FATF meeting. Market witnessed many important financial result 
announcements today that includes NCL, NML, FCCL, HTL and MEBL which were in line with market expectations. 
• 
The Index closed at 45,362.85pts as against 45,675.87pts showing a decrease of 313.02pts (-0.69% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (-
64.7pts), Cement (-45.3pts), E&P (-36.7pts), Technology (-31.1pts) and Power (-25.3pts).  
• 
Volumes decreased from 194.1mn shares to 137.7mn shares (-29.1% DoD). Traded value also decreased by 16.3% to reach US$ 20.6mn as against US$ 24.6mn.  
• 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include WTL, HUMNL, KEL, SNGP and BOP

• 
Range bound session was witnessed today due to the concerns over mounting international oil prices and rising Covid-19 cases. Market opened on a positive note but 
soon after profit taking was witnessed across the board. In the cement sector, LUCK closed in the red zone due to disappointing result as gross margin underwent a dip to 
22.6% during 2QFY22 (2QFY21: 29.2%) amid higher coal prices, PKR depreciation and augmented packaging costs, coupled with volumetric decline. Main board activity 
remained gloomy. On the flip-side, activity continued to remain side-ways as market witnessed hefty volumes in the 3rd tier stocks.  
• 
The Index closed at 45,077.91pts as against 45,083.04pts showing a decrease of 5.13pts (-0.01% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Cement (-
38.8pts), E&P (-17.8pts), Chemical (-12.6pts), OMC’S (-11.24pts) and Automobile Assembler (-3.11pts).  
•  Volumes increased from 173.6mn shares to 258.9mn shares (+49.2% DoD). Traded value also increased by 23.7% to reach US$ 46.0mn as against US$ 37.2mn.  
•  Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include TRG, HUMNL, UNITY, WTL and CNERGY.

 
Index stayed in the green zone ahead of mini-budget. Cement sector remained in the limelight as cement stocks rallied in the first trading hour due to reduction in 
international coal prices. In banking sector, MEBL stayed in the limelight as SBP for the first time has introduced shariah compliant liquidity facilities for Islamic banking 
institutions to enable them to offer better returns and rates to their customers on deposits and loans. Moreover, activity continued to remain side-ways as market witnessed 
hefty volumes in the 3rd tier stocks.  
 
The Index closed at 44,416pts as against 44,260pts showing an increase of 156.09pts (+0.35% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Technology & 
Communication (+79pts), Commercial Banks (+60pts), Fertilizer (+44pts) Cement (+32pts) and Tobacco (+16pts).  


 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include KEL, TREET, TRG, CNERGY and PRL.

 
KSE-100 index witnessed a bull-run as it gained more than 1,200 points in intraday trading to cross 45,000 points today, lifted by the cabinet approval to revive Saudi 
Arabia's $3 billion support package for Pakistan in safe deposits and $1.2bn worth of oil supplies on deferred payments. Major dip in crude oil prices created positive

created opportunity for intra-day traders.  


(+283pts), Cement (+211pts), E7P (+139pts), Fertilizer (+100pts) and OMC’s (+76pts).

 As the news of resumptions of dialogue with IMF team relayed, investors’ concerns over the package started dissipating, causing the Index to make an upward swing. The index 
posted a total gain of 934pts, closing the index +850pts (unadjusted). Cement, Technology, Banks, Fertilizer stocks remained in the limelight. Cement stocks led the index on 
news of Cement companies increasing the cement price / bag, whereas dwindling outflows from foreign counters in Banks and Fertilizer stocks also helped these sectors post 
healthy gains. Among scrips, WTL led the volumes with 49.3M shares, followed by HUMNL (24.7M) and BYCO (15.6M). 
 The Index closed at 45,500pts as against 44,629pts showing an increase of 870pts (+1.9% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (+201pts), Cement 
(+160pts), Technology (+106pts), Fertilizer (+79pts) and Textile (+49pts).  
 Volumes increased from 248.2mn shares to 308.2mn shares (+24% DoD). Average traded value also increased by 18% to reach US$ 59.8mn as against US$ 50.8mn.  
 Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include WTL, HUMNL, BYCO, TELE and UNITY, which formed 39% of total volumes.

Index Contribution

Source: PSX
 
 
Resolution of dispute between the members of ruling party as well as anticipation of tax incentives for the industries in the upcoming budget helped KSE100 index put up a 
formidable addition of 374pts during the session and ended the session +332pts (unadjusted). Deferral of OGDC and PPL for consideration of privatization resulted in a 
rebound in stock prices of both stocks. Banks, Cement and Steel sectors also contributed positively to the Index. The incentives  proposal for the refinery sector gave way to 
NRL, ATRL and PRL to post gains. Among volumes leaders, WTL led the table with 99.2M shares, followed by BYCO (67.5M) and HASCOL (48.3M). 
 
The Index closed at 45,915pts as against 45,574pts showing an increase of +341pts (+0.7% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (+116pts), E& 
(+68pts), Cement (+31pts), O&GMCs (+29pts) and Autos (+29pts).  
 
Volumes declined slightly from 784mn shares to 710.4mn shares (-10% DoD). Average traded value also declined by 6% to reach US$ 128.2mn as against US$ 135.7mn.  
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include WTL, BYCO, HASCOL, KEL and SMBL, which formed 41% of total volumes. 
 
Stocks that contributed positively to the index include HBL (+32pts), PPL (+31pts), OGDC (+28pts), UBL (+25pts) and PSO (+20pts). Stocks that contributed negatively 
include HASCOL (-8pts), PIBTL (-6pts), COLG (-4pts), TRG (-4pts) and PSX (-4pts).

• No peace deal over the weekend saw the KSE-100 retreat -0.69% DoD to 169.5k 
• 30 shares rose while 69 fell with HBL PA (+1.28%), MEBL PA (+0.58%) and THALL PA (+5.61%) contributing

• The IMF Executive Board is expected to approve more than USD 1.2bn for Pakistan as part of the broader 
USD 7bn EFF and RSF arrangements 
• Some additional weakness can be seen following the increase in rates but we expected Friday’s low @

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
March 26, 2026 
• The KSE-100 slumped -3.41% and gave up all of Wednesday’s gains 
• 7 shares rose while 92 fell with UBL PA (-7.07%), FFC PA (-2.84%) and ENGROH PA (-3.97%) the biggest 
index drags  
• Deputy PM Ishaq Dar said that US-Iran indirect talks are taking place through messages being relayed by 
Pakistan 
• The US and Iran are offering conflicting comments on efforts to end the war which did not allow follow through 
buying  
• Failure to regain the 200-DMA keeps the market in consolidation mode and the KSE-100 is flat WoW heading 
into the week’s last session 
• The government decided to launch a mobile app to manage fuel quotas for motorcycles, rickshaws, and 
potentially small cars (up to 800cc). The goal is to provide a direct subsidy to low-income drivers while using 
higher prices to encourage everyone to save fuel 
• Middle East news flow remains the main driver of markets with any positive development to lift equity markets 
significantly higher

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
September 25, 2025 
• 
Upside target of 159.3k hit in the week’s penultimate session  
• 
47 shares rose while 50 fell with HUBC PA (+6.34%), ENGROH PA (+3.59%), HBL PA (+3.71%) 
contributing the most to index gains  
• 
LUCK PA (-2.15%), OGDC PA (-1.76%), UBL PA (-0.61%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
US President Trump is scheduled to meet Pakistan’s PM Shehbaz at the White House later today, 
the latest sign of improving ties between the two nations 
• 
The power circular debt resolution agreement was signed yesterday, a PKR 1.225trn loan from banks 
at KIBOR minus 0.9%. This is likely to lower costs by 1.5–5%, as the facility will replace higher-cost 
obligations, including IPPs’ penal income (3M KIBOR + 200–450 bps) and PHPL interest charges 
(KIBOR + 2%), which will be refinanced 
• 
Heading into the last session of the week the KSE-100 is currently +0.79% and the weekly objective 
of 159.3k has been met

       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
August 27, 2025 
• 
5th consecutive down close for the KSE-100 which declined into the 147.5k level (LoD: 147.3k) with some panic 
in the second half of the session following news of major flooding in the north of the country  
• 
21 shares rose while 78 fell with MEBL PA (+0.95%), ENGROH PA (+0.61%) and MCB PA (+0.79%) contributing 
the most to index gains  
• 
HBL PA (-1.38%), FATIMA (-5.21%) and SRVI PA (-6.42%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
Pakistan plans to return to international capital markets either this year or next year with Panda Bonds the most 
likely avenue according to FinMin Aurangzeb who will be accompanying PM Sharif to attend the SCO conference 
in China next week 
• 
GoP is set to impose a 40% tax on the import of used cars. Under the IMF deal, Pakistan is bound to cut its 
import tariffs to 9.7% from 20.2% over five years. Pakistan imports of used cars in FY26 are projected to surge 
more than 40% to about 62,000 units 
• 
Heavy monsoon rains have triggered flash floods across Pakistan, PM Sharif held an emergency meeting on 
heavy rains in Punjab. Pakistan’s meteorological department warned of intense rainfall in the next 12 to 24 hours 
in Lahore, Gujranwala, Gujarat and Rawalpindi. Sialkot, a manufacturing hub in Punjab province, recorded 
363.5mm of rain in the past 24 hours, the most in 49 years. 
• 
With 147.5k having been struck the KSE-100 has retraced 70% of last weeks rally and in doing so now come into 
and optimal trade entry zone from where a recovery should be anticipated

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
June 25, 2025 
• 
The KSE-100 built on yesterday’s surge with a +0.42% gain to close in on the June highs 
• 
55 shares rose while 41 fell with BAHL PA (+4.15%), HBL PA (+3.03%) and FATIMA PA (+6.54%) 
contributing the most to index gains  
• 
HUBC PA (-1.93%), UBL PA (-0.71%) and ENGROH PA (-0.6%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
Regarding the US Trade Talks FinMin Aurangzeb said “Both sides showed satisfaction on the ongoing 
negotiations and resolved to conclude the trade negotiations next week” 
• 
ADB and WB have approved a total of USD 544mn in loans for Women-Inclusive Finance Sector 
Development Program. The funding includes a USD 300mn policy based loan and a USD 50mn financial 
intermediation loan designed to fund credit facilities. The WB approved USD 194mn funding for 
education and water projects in Pakistan’s Southwest Balochistan province 
• 
Weekly charts are looking solid heading in to the last few sessions of the week and 130K remains the 
current upside target

       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
May 27, 2025 
• 
Flat session ahead of the May 28 holiday  
• 
48 shares rose while 50 fell with MEBL PA (+1.61%), SYS PA (+1.22%) and PKGP PA (+6.77%) contributed the 
most to index gains  
• 
UBL PA (-1.78%), HUBC PA (-0.82%) and PSEL PA (-3.5%) were the biggest contributors to index declines  
• 
Headline inflation for May’25 is projected to rise to +3.04% YoY, an uptick from +0.3% YoY recorded in 
Apr’25, but still significantly lower than 11.8% YoY in May’24. The sharp YoY moderation is reflective of a 
strong base effect and easing inflationary pressures across key categories such as food and transportation. 
• 
Cement was the standout sector with FLYING PA (+10.0%) DCL PA (+8.29%) and DGKC PA (+3.23%) 
• 
The market appears to be biding its time for a positive catalyst to breach 120k on a sustained basis

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
April 16, 2025 
• 
Failure to regain the “Monday Tariff Gap” and displacement today on the downside  
• 
25 shares rose while 71 fell UBL PA (+0.87%), EFERT PA (+1.28%) and MCB PA (+0.94%) were the biggest 
contributors to gains  
• 
MARI PA (-2.51%), PPL PA (-1.75%) and PSO (-2.43%) were the largest index drags  
• 
UBL PA announced 1QCY25 EPS: 29.34, +125% YoY and DPS: 11. Alongside the results, UBL also announced 
a 2-for-1 stock split. Earnings were driven by a substantial YoY rise in Net-Interest Income of 200% along with 
provisioning reversal of PKR 1.6bn during the quarter 
• 
Pakistan will offer to buy more cotton and soybean from the US, as it looks to halve its bilateral trade surplus and 
escape US tariffs 
• 
Displacement away from the gap (117.6k-118.6k) is negative with HoD printing at 117.4k, further supporting our 
bearish near term bias

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
January 27, 2025 
• 
Declines to kick off the week with the KSE-100 declining -1.18% DoD ahead of the SBP MPC meeting where the 
SBP cut rates by 1% to 12%  
• 
23 shares rose while 75 fell with SYS PA (+3.07%), EFERT PA (+1.62%) and POL PA (+1.38%) contributing the 
most to index gains  
• 
MARI PA (-8.23%), PSO PA (-3.8%) and HUBC PA (-2.07%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
POL PA (+1.38%) announced 1HFY25 EPS: PKR 35.73, -42% YoY amid higher exploration expense associated 
with a dry well during the period. On a quarterly basis, EPS: PKR 26.68, -4% YoY. In addition to the result, the 
company announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 25.00/share in 2QFY25 
• 
MARI PA announced 1HFY25 EPS: PKR 25.32, -19% YoY. On a quarterly basis EPS: PKR 9.30, -39% YoY owing 
to i) 115% YoY surge in operating and administration cost, and ii) incremental 15% royalty on the wellhead value of 
the Mari D&P Lease from Nov'24. No dividend was announced  
• 
With the MPC now out of the way Tuesday is expected to upside resume for the KSE-100 and regain 115k

•

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
February 15, 2024 
 
• 
The KSE-100 turned lower as the political temperature remained elevated  
• 
Volatility remains very high as news headlines dominate  
• 
Only 14 stocks rose while 78 fell with OGDC PA (-6.47%), PPL PA (-4.58%), and HUBC (-1.94%) the biggest index 
decliners  
• 
MEBL PA (+0.18%) 4QCY23 EPS: PKR 15.00, +65% YoY, DPS: PKR 8. CY23 EPS 47.72, +90 YoY and  DPS: PKR 
20. The jump in overall earnings was mainly on the back of growth in total income 
• 
PSO PA (-4.55%) 1HFY24 EPS: PKR 16.51 vs LPS: 7.16 in the SPLY, 2QFY24 LPS: PKR 30.12 vs LPS: PKR 9.71 in 
the SPLY amid higher inventory loss 
• 
Draw on liquidity remains @ 59k with supply overhead @ 63-64k  
• 
February is again proving to be a weak seasonal period for the KSE-100

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
January 26, 2024 
 
• 
64k gave way which is the first indication that the seasonally weak period for the KSE-100 has begun. 
• 
WoW the KSE-100 gained +0.84% having been +3.05% at the week’s highs of +65k. 
• 
A/D again skewed to decliners at 22:71 with MARI PA (-3.25%), FFC PA (-2.45%), and MEBL PA (-1.49%) the biggest 
drags on the index. 
• 
OGDC PA (+4.48%), HBL PA (+1.75%), and INDU PA (+1.79%) were the biggest contributors to gains. 
• 
For the coming week if 64k solidifies as resistance the near term downside objective will be 62.5k 
• 
The current range for the index is 62.5k-65.5k and the bias is to the downside below 64k

December 22, 2023 
 
• 
Big down week with the KSE-100 losing -6.7% WoW  
• 
The last session before a long weekend saw the KSE-100 fall another -1.58%. 
• 
Skewed A/D of 20:77  
• 
Key index upside movers were HMB PA (+1.09%), PABC PA (+3.32%), and ILP PA (+1.06%) while the biggest index decliners 
were PPL PA (-4.06%), HBL PA (-3.12%) and OGDC PA (-2.29%).  
• 
61k was low for the week and is likely to be tested again next week. A breach and close back above 61k will be bullish.  
• 
On a weekly level support is around the 60k level with resistance @ 64k.

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
June 23, 2023 
 
 The day began in the green for the PSX though the battle was lost to the bears as the opening session closed in the red.  The second session resumed  in the red and lost 
257.63 points in the intraday. The impeding IMF deal along with the political uncertainty kept the investors at bay as the session concluded in the negative zone with losing net 
86.73 points. Volumes remained dry in the mainboard while the 3rd tier equity remained as the volume board leaders. 
 The index closed at 40,065.32 down by 86.73pts (-0.22% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Technology & Communication (-43.0pts), Commercial Banks 
(-23.3pts), Power Generation & Distribution (-17.1pts), Inv. Banks / Inv. Cos. / Securities Cos. (-16.5pts) and Automobile Assembler (-16.4pts).  
 Volumes increased from 116.5mn shares to 136.95mn shares (+17.5% DoD). The average traded value also increased by 32.0% to USD 11.8mn as against USD 8.9mn. 
 Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are WTL, UNITY, TRG, PTL and WAVES.

 The PSX concluded the week with a mixed session. The market opened in the red zone and traded in the same zone with sluggish activity throughout the first trading 
owing to the rupee dollar parity and political instability. However, after the second session resumed the bears took charge the index plunged down 440.97 points as 
investors opted to square-up their positions due to the rollover week. volumes remained healthy as investors are optimist about the upcoming IMF teams visit for the 9th 
review of the Extended Fund Facility from the IMF Program. 
 The index closed at 40,450.53 pts, down by 396.00 pts (-0.97% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Cement (-75.4pts), Technology & Communications 
(-75.3pts), Fertilizer (-73.7pts), Banks (-35.4pts), and Autos (-30.9pts).  
 Volumes decreased from 364.1mn shares to 191.3mn shares (-47.5% DoD). The average traded value also decreased by 52.2% to USD 27.1mn as against USD 56.7mn. 
 Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes are PRL, CNERGY, FFL, WTL and KEL.

Pakistan

• 
A bloodbath session was witnessed at the PSX today. The State Bank of Pakistan unexpectedly raised interest rates by 100 basis points during the post-close session of the 
previous business day, shaking investors' confidence. As anticipated the benchmark KSE-100 index opened in the negative zone and shed 972.79 points in the intraday. 
Investor participation remained active throughout the day, with 3rd tier stocks seeing the most activity. 
• 
The Index closed at 42,071.34 pts, down by 865.39pts (-2.02% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Cement (-204.7pts), E&P’s (-114.8pts), Commercial 
Banks (-110.1pts), Fertilizer (-78.4pts), Technology & Communication (-53.5pts).

From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
August 29, 2022 
 
 
The market observed a negative session today due to concerns over mounting inflation and GDP growth rate. Investors' participation remained sideways throughout the day although 
value buying was witnessed in the last trading hour which helped the index to recover as it made an intraday low of 763.38 points. Volumes remained dry in the main board whereas 
hefty volumes were witnessed in the 3rd tier stocks. 
 
The Index closed at 42,504.34pts, down by 87.17pts (-0.20% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include E&P’s (-29.5pts), Food & Personal Care (-25.7pts), Autos (-
17.8pts), Pharmaceuticals (-15.9pts) and Textiles (-13.4pts).  


From Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
May 27, 2022 
 
 
The market witnessed a long-awaited bull run after the government finally announced to increase petroleum prices considering the IMF Program resumption which resulted in the price 
appreciation of Pak Rupee against USD.  The investors rejoiced over the news as KSE-100 went up by 1,013 points during the session giving bulls an upper hand to remain active 
throughout the day.  However, profit selling was observed in the last trading hour due to the end of Rollover week.  Hefty volumes were observed all across the board on the contrary 
3rd tier stocks remained in the limelight. 
 
The Index closed at 42,861.45pts, up by 319.74pts (+0.75% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Fertilizer (+44.1pts), E&P’s (+43.0pts), Banks (+40.5pts), Chemical 
(+37.6pts) and Cement (+35.4pts).  


• 
Range bound session was witnessed today due to concerns over rising Covid-19 cases and mounting international oil prices. Cement sector stayed in the red zone as 
provisional cement data for the month of Jan’22 depicts a decline of 19% YoY to 3.85mn tons whereby domestic dispatches recorded a dip of 18% YoY to 3.32mn tons led 
primarily by slowdown in construction activity. In fertilizer sector, FFBL made the journey to its lower circuit due to disappointing result announcement. Moreover, main 
board activity remained gloomy. On the flip-side, activity continued to remain side-ways as market witnessed hefty volumes in the 3rd tier stocks. In the last trading hour, 
value buying was observed due to announcement of corporate results. 
• 
The Index closed at 44,955.05pts as against 44,887.77pts showing an increase of 67.28pts (+0.15% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Technology 
(+15.1pts), Chemical (+25.8pts), Commercial Banks (+20.2pts), Inv. Banks (+14.8pts) and Cement (+10.6pts).  
• 
Volumes decreased from 207.0mn shares to 137.3mn shares (-33.7% DoD). Traded value also decreased by 33.9% to reach US$ 30.3mn as against US$ 45.9mn.  
• 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include TRG, GGL, TPLP, WTL and CNERGY.

Battle between bulls and bears was conquered by the bulls in the last trading hour. Market opened with a positive momentum as investors perceived that last leg of foreign

 Market dipped 701pts during the session today and closed the session -612pts (unadjusted). Financial year end closing for the institutional investors (Banks and Mutual Funds) 
prompted profit booking, especially in the aftermath of MSCI downgrade from Emerging Market to Frontier Market which can possibly result in selling from foreign corporates. 
Selling pressure was witnessed across the board with emphasis on Cement, Banks, E&P sectors. Among scrips, WTL topped the volumes with 97.7M shares, followed by 
BYCO (52.4M) and JSCLR1 (50.3M).  
 The Index closed at 47,002pts as against 47,603pts showing a decline of 601pts (-1.3% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (-191pts), Cement (-
106pts), O&GMCs (-53pts), E&P (-52pts) and Textile (-45pts).  
 Volumes declined from 761.4mn shares to 655.1mn shares (-14% DoD). Average traded value also declined by 13% to reach US$ 119.2mn as against US$ 137.5mn.  
 Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include WTL, BYCO, JSCLR1, KEL and TREET, which formed 39% of total volumes. 
 Stocks that contributed positively to the index include TRG (+22pts), ABOT (+10pts), THALL (+8pts), ABL (+8pts) and PKGS (+8pts). Stocks that contributed negatively include 
HBL (-69pts), LUCK (-51pts), UBL (-43pts), HUBC (-33pts) and MCB (-24pts).

 
Market traded range bound oscillating between +149pts and -163pts, ending the session -134pts (unadjusted). Political uncertainty continued playing with sentiments today 
besides selling in E&P stocks due to concern over crude oil price in international market and liquidity crunch affecting operational performance of underlying companies. 
Banking stocks followed suit with negative price performance. Besides, Technology stocks saw selling pressure in TRG that put additional pressure on the Index. BYCO, 
which saw significant share transfers in NDMs yesterday, realized healthy volumes with price uptick in the regular market, topping the volume leaders. HASCOL realized an 
upper circuit closing for the second consecutive day. Among scrips, BYCO led the table followed by WTL (77.7M) and HASCOL (59.5M). 
 
The Index closed at 45,574pts as against 45,682pts showing a decline of 108pts (-0.2% DoD). Sectors contributing to the performance include Banks (-102pts), E&P (-
48pts), Technology (-25pts), Cement (-13pts) and Engineering (-7pts).  
 
Volumes increased from 578.3mn shares to 784.0mn shares (+36% DoD). Average traded value on the other hand remained at US$ 135.7mn as against US$ 135.5mn the 
other day.  
 
Stocks that contributed significantly to the volumes include BYCO, WTL, HASCOL, KEL and PIBTL, which formed 45% of total volumes. 
 
Stocks that contributed positively to the index include PIBTL (+15pts), HASCOL (+14pts), SEARL (+13pts), COLG (+13pts) and DAWH (+9pts). Stocks that contributed 
negatively include HBL (-49pts), OGDC (-24pts), HMB (-21pts), MCB (-17pts) and SYS (-17pts).

From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
April 23, 2026 
• Declines into 170k saw some late demand having been under pressure throughout the session  
• 18 shares rose while 82 fell with FFC PA (-1.94%), UBL PA (-1.99%) and MEBL PA (-2.32%) the biggest 
index drags  
• Heavy day for Q1 earnings from banks with FABL PA (-1.22%) reporting Q1CY26 EPS: 3.51, -2% YoY and 
DPS: 1.5. BAFL PA (-4.25%) reported Q1CY26 EPS: 3.48, +55% YoY and DPS: 1.5. MEBL PA (-1.8%) 
reported Q1EPS: 12.39, +1% YoY an DPS: 7.5. MCB PA (-0.9%) reported Q1EPS: 11.07, -11% YoY and 
DPS: 9.0  
• Sitara Petroleum Service Limited has announced an IPO, seeking to raise up to PKR 4.8bn to finance 
expansion in its fuel retail network, logistics operations and storage infrastructure. The company is offering a 
total of 279.9 million ordinary shares, representing 16.66% of its post-IPO paid-up capital. The book building 
process is scheduled for May 4-5, followed by public subscription on May 11-12 with Arif Habib Limited acting 
as the lead manager and book runner to the issue 
• The US said it’s waiting for a response from Iran before the warring sides can restart peace talks, with the 
impasse and worsening tension over the Strait of Hormuz causing energy prices to rise again 
• Pakistan has taken a major step towards strengthening its energy security by forming a high level committee 
to design and operationalize Strategic Petroleum Reserves, aimed at reducing vulnerability to global oil price 
shocks and supply disruptions 
• Heading into the last session of the week the KSE-100 is currently -2.7% WoW and entering a support zone 
between 165-170k from where demand is anticipated to emerge

Minor gains to regain the 158k level for the KSE-100 in another flattish session  
• 
46 shares rose while 53 fell with PPL PA (+4.33%), HUBC PA (+2.75%) and UBL PA (+0.91%) 
contributing the most to index gains  
• 
FFC PA (-0.79%), SYS PA (-1.32%) and EFERT PA (-1.24%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
The KSE-100 index’s profitability (based on 93% of the index weightage) increased by 1.8% YoY in 
FY25, reaching PKR 1.66trn. This earnings increase was witnessed on the back of 205%, 119%, 
43%, 41, and 39% YoY in the Investment Banks, Pharmaceuticals, Miscellaneous, Cement and 
Auto Assemblers respectively. Conversely, decline was recorded in Textiles, Chemicals, Power, 
E&P and Insurance YoY decrease of 55%, 41%, 40%, 20% and 2% respectively 
• 
Upside target of 159.3k remains valid above 157k

• 
Additional downside to move deeper into support

43 shares rose while 57 fell with FFC PA (+1.19%), HUBC PA (+1.93%) and POL PA (+1.82%) 
contributed the most to index gains  
•

data (June 2024) stands at USD 7.75bn

PKR 7.725bn  
•

proposed a seven-year tariff control period to facilitate long-term investments. For FY24, the average

determined at PKR 1,348.66/kW/month

       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
March 13, 2025 
• 
Displacement to the upside was seen on Thursday with the KSE-100 taking out 115k to set up a move towards 
116k before the end of the week  
• 
69 shares rose while 28 fell with PSO PA (+4.56%), FFC PA (+1.32%), LUCK PA (+2.83%) contributed the most to 
index gains  
• 
SRVI PA (-2.55%), POL PA (-0.68%), BAFL PA (-0.83%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
Heading into the last session of the week the KSE-100 is currently +0.67% WoW and on track to have its 5th 
consecutive up week and 116k remains the draw for this week 
• 
Expect upside momentum to accelerate

## REPORT 195
Title: From the Trading Floor 13-Feb-25
Created At: 2025-02-13T11:25:50.359Z
Item ID: 67add6be8ad2dd7a1e1f321f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
February 13, 2025 
• 
Further consolidation with the KSE-100 managing to hold above 112k 
• 
41 shares rose while 53 fell with LUCK PA (+2.82%), PSEL PA (+6.72%) and MCB PA (+0.83%) contributing the 
most to index gains  
• 
BAHL PA (-4.57%), PPL PA (-2.66%), PSO PA (-2.95%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
PSO PA announced 1HFY25 EPS: PKR 23.81, up 3x YoY. On a quarterly basis PSO PA reported EPS: PKR 15.35 
in 2QFY25 against a LPS: PKR 30.11 in 2QFY24 amid inventory gains during the quarter coupled with lower finance 
cost. 
• 
MEBL announced CY24 EPS: PKR 57.28, +20% YoY and in 4QCY24 EPS: PKR 13.66, -8% YoY Along with the 
result, MEBL announced a final dividend of PKR 7/share (CY24: PKR 28/share). The jump in overall earnings was 
mainly on the back of growth in total income. Along with the result, MEBL announced a final dividend of PKR 7/share 
(CY24: PKR 28/share).  
• 
Gains on Friday above 113.5k can see upside acceleration

Title: From Trading Floor 24-Jan-2025
Created At: 2025-01-24T12:37:15.786Z
Item ID: 6793897b6ae6aa2b5392b796

       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
January 24, 2025 
• 
Decent recovery to close the week just shy of the 115k to limit WoW declines to -0.4% 
• 
53 shares rose while 44 fell with FFC PA (+3.63%), SYS PA (+2.69%) and EFERT PA (+1.78%) contributed the 
most to index gains 
• 
MARI PA (-1.79%), OGDC PA (-0.69%) and PPL PA (-0.49%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
SBP FX reserves declined USD 276mn in the week ended January 17 to USD 11.4bn 
• 
Monday’s MPC has the markets attention where we expect a 100-bps cut 
• 
Expectations for the coming week are for the KSE-100 to trade through 117k and set up a retest of all time highs  
•

## REPORT 197
Title: From the Trading Floor - 19-Dec-24
Created At: 2024-12-19T11:45:06.531Z
Item ID: 67640742ecfd7de5dd54aea3
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076

       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
December 19, 2024 
• 
When it rains it pours, the KSE-100 gave up another -4.32% DoD to take the 3-day losing streak to -9.2% 
• 
6 shares rose while 94 fell with MARI PA (-10.0%), HUBC PA (-8.38%) and UBL PA (-4.52%) the biggest 
downside contributors  
• 
INDU PA (+9.07%), RMPL PA (+3.92%) and AGP PA (+1.26%) provided the most upside index contributions  
• 
Beautiful price delivery to take out the equal lows @ 107.5k and then some. The market has now completed 5 
waves down and a “convincing” short term counter trend rally can now materialize  
• 
Counter trend rallies are likely be capped below 111.5k over the coming weeks  
•

## REPORT 200
Title: From The Trading Floor 23-Sep-24
Created At: 2024-09-23T12:29:11.375Z
Item ID: 66f15f170cbf3cea06d4cd4b
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c118b0ffa16ff64800076
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66f15f170cbf3cea06d4cd4b.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
       P a g e  1 | 2 
 
 
From the Trading Floor 
Pakistan  
Stock Market                                                    
September 23, 2024 
• 
Spill over selling from the FTSE rebal kept the market under pressure on Monday  
• 
40 shares rose while 59 fell with FFC PA (+7.0%), UBL PA (+3.4%) and EFERT PA (+2.78%) having the biggest 
upside index contributions  
• 
HUBC PA (-5.81%), MARI PA (-5.98%) and PPL PA (-2.93%) were the biggest index drags  
• 
OGDC PA (-1.9%) announced FY23 EPS: PKR 48.59, -7% YoY. Q4 EPS: PKR 8.81, -42% YoY, which were below 
expectations. Alongside the result, the company announced a final cash dividend of PKR 4.00/share (PKR 
10.10/share in FY24) 
• 
HTL PA (-7.37%) announced FY24 LPS: PKR 0.99 compared to loss of LPS: PKR 1.77) in FY23. On a quarterly 
basis, the company registered EPS: PKR 1.29) compared to a (LPS: PKR 0.48) in SPLY 
• 
KSE-100 is expected to find support around the 81k level from where 83k would be the near term target
